title: buildah pull failing, however podman pull working fine
body:
[root@localhost trilio-datamover-api]# buildah pull registry.redhat.io/rhosp15-rhel8/openstack-nova-api
Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.redhat.io/rhosp15-rhel8/openstack-nova-api:latest: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
[root@localhost trilio-datamover-api]# podman pull registry.redhat.io/rhosp15-rhel8/openstack-nova-api

comments.body: Logged in to redhat registry using 'podman login' command. I learnt that 'buildah' should fetch registry credentials from podman. But buidah pull is failing with credentials issue.

[root@localhost trilio-datamover-api]# podman info

No help topic for 'info'
[root@localhost trilio-datamover-api]# buildah --version
buildah version 1.5 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
[root@localhost trilio-datamover-api]#

@shyam-biradar thanks for the issue report.  Both the Podman and Buildah versions that you're using are very old.  We're currently at Buildah v1.11.3 and Podman 1.6.2 as the newest.  I know that since the versions that you're using, we've done a number of changes to the authentication process.  Would it be possible for you to update the software and try again?
@TomSweeneyRedHat  Thanks. I will upgrade both and will try pull commands again.

@TomSweeneyRedHat : I have installed both podman and buildah with default yum install commands.
I think both packages came from rhel8 distro. How can I upgrade them? Do we have any special yum repo rhel8?

RHEL8 version will not be updated until RHEL8.1 (Next month),  After that we should be updating every three months.  There was release problems, which have prevented us from updating sooner.  As you can imaginge Podman and Buildah are moving very fast...

@rhatdan : Thank you for this information. Any other way to install latest podman and buildah on rhel8?
Manual way from github?
@lsm5 @jnovy Do you know of a way?
This is fixed in latest rhel8 content.
